title: SOI - BD FACSAria Fusion Cell Sorter - Plate Sorting
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dohgu","text":"This Standard Operation Instruction is intended to guide the facility users to set-up and perform plate sorting on a BD FACSAria Fusion™ instrument","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7eksm","text":"This protocol should be done by:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hph1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8jn5c","text":"The facility staff.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"41vnp","text":"Trained users of the FCCF.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b86ji","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7o281","text":"This protocol should be performed:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qjr","text":"Every time one wants to perform plate sorting in the cell sorter.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a4nfr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dh75q","text":"Perform the steps subsequently unless otherwise stated.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"6mjgh","text":"Perform a Fluidics Startup (SOI - BDFACSAria Fusion Cell Sorter - Instrument Startup).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ir0k","text":"Make sure the stream is adjusted and stable.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ar7k9","text":"Create an experiment, adjust the PMT gains and run compensation (if necessary).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ck2ek","text":"Create a gating strategy to reach target population.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"1pf4q","text":"Splash shield","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fch4m","text":"PCR plate holder (if sorting on PCR plates or strips)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d6n93","text":" ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"10prq","text":"96 well plate covered with plastic foil","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fhgbk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":960744,"guid":"110D8E055D6A11EE80E00A58A9FEAC02","name":"BD FACS™ Accudrop Beads","mol_weight":0,"sku":"345249","url":"https://www.bdbiosciences.com/en-us/products/reagents/flow-cytometry-reagents/clinical-discovery-research/controls-and-supporting-reagents-ruo-gmp/accudrop-beads.345249","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":1503,"name":"Becton Dickinson (BD)","link":"https://www.bd.com/en-us","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Make sure the sort block chamber is closed. Remove the tube collection device, if installed.
Insert the splash shield into the slotted fittings below the sort block chamber and push it all the way in. Please note that the magnet on the splash shield must be facing the upper right position
To bring the ACDU tray (Automated Cell Deposition Unit) to the front of the instrument, click the Access Stage button in the sort layout.

Open the Drop Delay experiment (FCCF → Set-up → Drop Delay). Expand "Global Worksheets" and "PLEASE DON'T EDIT THESE SORT LAYOUTS!!!". Double click on the correspondent sort layout to open it according to your plate option: The available options are the following: 6 wells 12 wells 24 wells 96 wells custom (100 µM) - Partial 96 wells custom (130 µM) - Partial 384 wells custom - Partial

Pull the lever to the left and place over the ACDU tray a test plate related to the plates you are going to sort. The A1 position should be toward the front of the stage on the left side. The sorting order is A1 to the back of the plate and then from left to right and back to the front, in a zig zag motion. This means that a full 96 wells plate will have A1 as first well and H12 as last.
Open the Sort Device Control.
On "Device, select "ACDU". Do NOT change any other settings! Close the software.
Collapse the left, right and far right side streams.
On the side stream window, activate Voltage. The icon will become red to indicate that the deflection plates are being charged.
Activate Test Sort.
Go to Sort → Custom Devices.
Adjust the far left side stream until it is in the centre of the the two left parallel bars on the side stream camera. Finally, switch off Voltage.
Find the custom option for the device you want to sort into.
Click on Go to Home. The tray is going to move to the home position.
Activate Voltage in the side stream window.
Double click the Test Sort button in the Device Setup window to deposit a drop over the plate at the home location. Need a screenshot with the test sort activated
Check the plate to see if the drop was deposited in the centre of the well.

If necessary, adjust the coordinates for the home location. Click on the arrows to move the tray to the desired position. Please note that when you are clicking on the arrows, what it is moving is the tray with the plate, and not the stream! This means that for instance - if a drop is too much to the right of the well - the tray has to be moved to the right so that the drop is deposited more to the left. The small arrows move the tray by one step and the large arrows by 5 steps.
Repeat go to step #3.3 through go to step #3.6 until the deposited drop is in the centre of the well.
Click on Set Home. Be careful to NOT click on Set Farthest!!!
Click on Go Farthest. The plate will move to the farthest well from A1.
Repeat go to step #3.3 through go to step #3.6 until the deposited drop is in the centre of the well.
Click on Set Farthest.

In the Drop Delay Experiment, double click to open the sort layout for the device you just have set. If testing a 96 or 384 wells plate, one can test first the "Partial" layout to speed up the time of sorting for checking.
Switch off the voltage.
Put the tube with BD FACS™ Accudrop Beads Becton Dickinson (BD) Catalog #345249 into the loading port. Load the tube.
Click on "Sort". A Confirm dialog will follow. Press OK to confirm.
Check if the drops are in the centre of the wells. If not, repeat go to step #3 to go to step #4.2 until the drops are properly placed.
creator: Daniel Gimenes
